texto: Your father...isn't good with emotions.” “Yeah. Figured that out a while ago.” Like, when I was four and cried because our family cat died and he offered to have it stuffed as a means to make me feel better. It didn't.

texto: Why are you in my room?”“Because I can be.”“You shouldn’t be.”“Save it, Rochester. You broke my nose.”“Does it hurt?”He lifted a hand toward his face and dropped it. “You could say that.”“Good.”He nudged a tray on the floor with his boot. It had oatmeal, toast, and orange juice on it. “Hungry?”Honor’s stomach growled. “No.”Ryder’s lips turned up in a fleeting sadistic smile. He kicked the tray across the room. It hit the wall and overturned. “Good.

You broke my n…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre desenvolvimento-pessoal, action, adventure, com foco em adventure e science-fiction.

texto: Sometimes in life, you do things you don’t want to. Sometimes you sacrifice, sometimes you compromise. Sometimes you let go and sometimes you fight. It’s all about deciding what’s worth losing and what’s worth keeping.
explicacao: Citação de Lindy Zart sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Sometimes in life, you do things you don’t want to. Sometimes you sacrifice, sometimes you compro…” articula autoestima, compromise, love em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como love e sacrifice.
